title: Stisseria × multiflora
taxonomy: Stisseria × multiflora is a hybrid species within the genus Stisseria, which is commonly known as the black magic vine. This plant belongs to the family Apocynaceae, a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The Stisseria genus is native to Australia and New Zealand, and the species is often found in tropical and subtropical regions. As a hybrid, Stisseria × multiflora's classification is complex and not well-defined, with some sources considering it a variant of Stisseria stipulata. Further research is needed to fully understand the classification of Stisseria × multiflora.
uses: Stisseria × multiflora has been found to have various medicinal uses in traditional medicine, particularly in Africa. The plant is used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Its leaves and stems are also used to make infusions and decoctions that are believed to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
